filename: Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx
relative_path: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Info package for your Helsinki visit, July 2022.docx
full_text:
Welcome to Helsinki Alan and Bonnie!

In this info-package you can find a schedule of hosted events, responsibility persons and their contact-info and, in addition, some tips for your trip. We hope you find Helsinki pleasant and enjoy your holiday here!

Friday evening / Saturday morning

Before leaving the hotel or first thing when arriving at Kauppatori if not using public transport to get there - Elina is glad to help then if needed!

You might like to consider downloading app for buying public transportation (HSL) tickets and for seeing public transportation routes:

->for Apple HSL App Storessa

->for Android HSL - tickets & route planner - Apps on Google Play

For your stay, we recommend a 4 day ticket as it is the most convenient. 4-day-ticket is 20€ per adult and paid in the app from the debit/card.

The app is available in English. If app downloads in Finnish, you can easily change settings from “Lisää” next to three lines-icon, “Asetukset”->”Kieli”->English

See instructions for buying the tickets here, click: Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app

Saturday 2.7. Trip to Suomenlinna

Responsible persons: Alfredo, Fernando and Elina (+358407494247)

UPDATE: Alfredo, Fernando and Elina will pick you up from your hotel 11.30

Meeting point: Kauppatori, Eteläranta, 00170 Helsinki in front of famous Havis Amanda statue. Click here to see location: Havis Amanda

TIP: we recommend to take a route to Kauppatori such that you can walk along Bulevardi and Esplanadin puisto (Esplanadi park), click: Recommended route to walk to Kauppatori

At Kauppatori, souvenirs, morning coffee, and ice cream etc. are available. Be aware of seagulls if eating something.

Boat 19 to Suomenlinna departs from Kauppatori (see map below)

Departure times are 20 and 50 past hour (i.g. 8:20 and 8:50) (click here for full boat schedule: Linja 19) Boat trip takes around 15 minutes.

HSL day ticket AB covers that trip, tickets are shown to a machine in the terminal before entering the boat.

Suomenlinna is an important historical island at the front of Helsinki that has a lot to offer. There is a port, stony fortitude and several restaurants. Here from official website can be read more of Suomenlinna: Suomenlinna (in English)

They have a guided tour at the fortitude to discover the area's history in English 13:30 from Suomenlinnakeskus (address: Suomenlinna C 74, 00190 Helsinki), its duration approx. 1h. Tickets to guided tour for adults 12€/person, reserved from this page: Suomenlinna tours reservation

Suomenlinna has several suitable restaurants, open as follows:

Locations and more info can be found from, click: Suomenlinna page (in English). Some notes: Bastion Bistro is perhaps the finest of these, Valimo is more family-friendly but still nice looking, Deli&Cafe serves lunch and cafeteria food and Pizzeria Nikolai serves fine pizzas.

Sunday 3.7.

Anniina will take you to beautiful Porvoo - a city of wooden architecture, lovely cafés and boutiques! See more about Porvoo here, click: Visit Porvoo (English)

Meeting point: Anniina picks you up with her car from your hotel around 10.

For the evening, Anniina has reserved a table from restaurant Saari 18:30, to where you arrive together by boat from Merisatamanranta 10, 00150 Helsinki. More info of restaurant, click: Ravintola Saari (English)

4.7.

Trip to Seurasaari + visit the Färkkilä lab and Meilahti campus

If you, Bonnie, don't want to join in for the afternoon in the lab (though we would be very happy to have you here!), there are several suggestions of great activities and places to visit at the end of this infopackage that you might then consider. Special recommendation for Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden + Cafe Viola from Suvi!

Tuesday 5.7.

Helsinki-tour

Recommended activity for Tuesday evening to you two:

Sauna at Löyly - visitor-friendly Sauna at Helsinki, amazing view and terrace

Address: Hernesaarenranta 4, 00150 Helsinki

More about Löyly, click Löyly (in English)

Sauna slots are 2h, one can stay up to that time. Please notice that Löyly requires using swimwear in sauna (rent possible, cost 6 euros per person)

Sauna entrance 21€ person (includes towel, pefletti* and soap). Booking is recommended to be done beforehand from here: Sauna reservation - Ravintola Löyly Helsinki Notice: Reservation has to be paid in 10 min after making it

They also have a restaurant at Löyly, however during summertime booking is not possible - first come first served

*pefletti is a small towel (paper or fabric made) on which one sits in sauna

Tips of great activities to do on your own at Helsinki if you have time&energy:

visit:

National natural museum, open daily from Mon.­–Sun. from 10 am to 5 pm. Address: Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13, 00100 Helsinki. More info and prices: https://www.luomus.fi/en/natural-history-museum

Kiasma (national museum of modern art). Address: Mannerheiminaukio 2, 00100 Helsinki. Schedule, info, prices and tickets: https://kiasma.fi/en/ Please notice that Kiasma (as many other museums) is closed on Mondays.

Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden: The outdoor garden, covering approximately four hectares, attracts the visitor to experience the world of plants. In summertime, perhaps the most attractive part of the grounds is the central garden, an open area with ponds and flowers, bordered by glasshouses (picture) and rose bushes. A historic stone building envisioned as the king’s castle now houses a herbarium in the middle of the garden.

Address: Kaisaniemenranta 2

00170 Helsinki. Entrance to the area is free of charge, entrance price to greenhouses is 12€ for adults. Official web page, click Kaisaniemi Botanic Garden | LUOMUS

Meilahti arboretum (flower park somewhat near Meilahti campus). Free of Charge. Address: Johannesbergintie 7, 00250 Helsinki

eat&drink:

If you visit Kaisaniemen Kasvitieteellinen puutarha, be sure to eat in Cafe Viola (address Kaisaniemenranta 2, 00170 Helsinki). It is located right next to Kaisaniemen kasvitieteellinen puutarha and open 10-18 weekdays. The place is awesome, warm recommendation from Suvi who has visited it several times.

Kahvila Rakastan: fully plant-based, traditional Finnish kitchen inspired cafée: https://kahvilarakastan.fi/herkut/ Address: Mannerheimintie 13 B,

00100 Helsinki

Kiasma has also cafeteria: Kiasma Cafe serves freshly made lunch and cafeteria bites. (Mannerheiminaukio 2, 00100 Helsinki)

Cafe Engel is a historical place and very beautiful cafe in the heart of Helsinki, next to Senaatintori. Address: Aleksanterinkatu 26, 00170 Helsinki. Open from 8. https://www.cafeengel.fi/

Restaurant Sunn. Nordic kitchen, lovely view to Senaatintori and beautiful setting. Address: Aleksanterinkatu 26, 2.krs, 00170 Helsinki. https://ravintolasunn.fi/

We hope you will enjoy your trip to Helsinki!

We are happy to answer any possible questions and help you, so please do not hesitate to ask us.

See then i.g.

Pikku-Finlandia restaurant (soup lunch)
or Kahvila Rakastan (plant-based cafeteria) are near visiting area | | Elina | Pikku-Finlandia address: Karamzininranta 4, 00100 Helsinki

12.45- 14.30 | The national history museum

Afternoon café at museum’s indoor yard café | address: Mannerheimintie 34, 00100 Helsinki | Suvi (+358453400875) | 1 h 45 min visiting time, it is recommended to reserve tickets online, click Opening hours and tickets - The national museum 

Excellent museum, at least visit to permanent exhibition of Finnish history (covering years of independence): https://www.kansallismuseo.fi/en/exhibitions/suomen-tarina
